CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE OF OCT-28-2020 CONTINUED TO DEC-09-2020 AT 10:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 610 FOR PLAINTIFF TO OBTAIN DEFAULT JUDGMENT AGAINST DEFENDANT(S). NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT PLAINTIFF CALIFORNIA BARREL COMPANY LLC. A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY RECOVER FROM DEFENDANT ALL PERSON CLAIMING ANY INTEREST OR LIEN UPON THE REAL PROPERTY HEREIN DESCRIBED OR, ANY PART THEREOF SEE SCANNED DOC - IT IS ORDERED, ADJUDGED AND DECREED THAT: (1) THAT PLAINTIFF BRINGS THIS ACTION IN REM AGAINST ALL THE WORLD TO ESTABLISH TITLE TO THE PROPERTY AND TO DETERMINE ALL ADVERSE CLAIMS THERETO SUBJECT TO THE EXISTING PARCEL ONE EXCEPTED INTERESTS, PARCEL TWO EXCEPTED INTERESTS, PARCEL THREE EXCEPTED INTERESTS, PARCEL FOUR EXCEPTED INTERESTS, PARCEL FIVE EXCEPTED INTERESTS AND PARCEL SIX EXCEPTED INTERESTS; (2) THAT NO VALID JUDGMENT HAS HERETOFORE BEEN ENTERED AS TO THE PROPERTY UNDER THE AUTHORITY OF THE ACT ENTITLED "[A]N ACT TO PROVIDE FOR THE ESTABLISHMENT AN QUIETING OF TITLE TO REAL PROPERTY IN THE CASE OFLOSS OR DESTRUCTION OF PUBLIC RECORDS" APPROVED JUNE 16, 1906, AND AS AMENDED AND MOST RECENTLY CODIFIED IN CCP SECTION 751.02, ET SEQ, THAT NO VALID JUDGMENT HAS BEEN ENTERED IN ANY ACTION AUTHORIZED BY CHAPTER 3.5 OF PART 2, TITLE 10 OF THE CCP; (3) THAT PLAINTIFF HAS COMPLIED WITH THE PROVISIONS OF THE DESTROYED LAND RECORDS ACT, CCP SECTION 751.02, ET SEQ; AND (4) THAT THE PROPERTY DESCRIPTIONS ATTACHED AS EXHIBIT A TO THE PROPOSED JUDGMENT ARE TRUE AND CORRECT PROPERTY DESCRIPTIONS OF PARCELS ONE THROUGH SIX.

THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT PLAINTIFF CALIFORNIA BARREL COMPANY LLC. A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY RECOVER FROM DEFENDANT ALL PERSON CLAIMING ANY INTEREST OR LIEN UPON THE REAL PROPERTY HEREIN DESCRIBED OR, ANY PART THEREOF SEE SCANNED DOC - IT IS ORDERED, ADJUDGED AND DECREED THAT: (1) THAT PLAINTIFF BRINGS THIS ACTION IN REM AGAINST ALL THE WORLD TO ESTABLISH TITLE TO THE PROPERTY AND TO DETERMINE ALL ADVERSE CLAIMS THERETO SUBJECT TO THE EXISTING PARCEL ONE EXCEPTED INTERESTS, PARCEL TWO EXCEPTED INTERESTS, PARCEL THREE EXCEPTED INTERESTS, PARCEL FOUR EXCEPTED INTERESTS, PARCEL FIVE EXCEPTED INTERESTS AND PARCEL SIX EXCEPTED INTERESTS; (2) THAT NO VALID JUDGMENT HAS HERETOFORE BEEN ENTERED AS TO THE PROPERTY UNDER THE AUTHORITY OF THE ACT ENTITLED "[A]N ACT TO PROVIDE FOR THE ESTABLISHMENT AN QUIETING OF TITLE TO REAL PROPERTY IN THE CASE OFLOSS OR DESTRUCTION OF PUBLIC RECORDS" APPROVED JUNE 16, 1906, AND AS AMENDED AND MOST RECENTLY CODIFIED IN CCP SECTION 751.02, ET SEQ, THAT NO VALID JUDGMENT HAS BEEN ENTERED IN ANY ACTION AUTHORIZED BY CHAPTER 3.5 OF PART 2, TITLE 10 OF THE CCP; (3) THAT PLAINTIFF HAS COMPLIED WITH THE PROVISIONS OF THE DESTROYED LAND RECORDS ACT, CCP SECTION 751.02, ET SEQ; AND (4) THAT THE PROPERTY DESCRIPTIONS ATTACHED AS EXHIBIT A TO THE PROPOSED JUDGMENT ARE TRUE AND CORRECT PROPERTY DESCRIPTIONS OF PARCELS ONE THROUGH SIX.

Request For Prove-Up Hearing Re Application For Court Judgment For Quiet Title
Off Calendar Oct-01-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendant "All Persons Claiming any Interest or Lien Upon the Real Property Herein Described..."
Dept. 514. Judge: Gerardo C. Sandoval. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty. Reporter: Ann Mendoza, CSR #6812. Telephone: 415-551-0625. Email: amendoza@sftc.org.

Gerald M. Murphy. appeared for plaintiff California Barrel Company LLC, a Delaware limited liability company.
Enrique Landa, Manager at California Barrel, Jim Benson, Title Officer at First American Title Company, and Sabrina Pack, Land Surveyor, were placed under oath and testified.
Mr. Landa, Mr. Benson and Ms. Pack all appeared via CourtCall for today's proceedings.
No appearance by defendant "All Persons Claiming any Interest or Lien Upon the Real Property Herein Described..."
Plaintiff's Second Amended Complaint for Quiet Title Under Destroyed Land Records Act was filed on May 16, 2019.
Lis Pendens was filed on April 16, 2019.
Initial Proof of Publication was filed on August 6, 2019, with publication dates of March 14, 2019, March 21, 2019, March 28, 2019, April 4, 2019 and April 11, 2019.
Additional Proof of Publication was filed on August 6, 2019, with publication dates of April 18, 2019 and April 25, 2019.
Default was entered on September 5, 2019.

Plaintiff California Barrel Company LLC submits the following exhibits for the Court's review:
Exhibit 1: Title Company Report.......................................................ID/EVID
Exhibit 2: Diagram of six parcels which comprise the property.........................ID/EVID

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of plaintiff California Barrel Company LLC, a Delaware limited liability company, and against the defaulted defendant "All Persons Claiming any Interest or Lien Upon the Real Property Herein Described..." as follows:

(1) That plaintiff brings this action in rem against all the world to establish title to the Property and to determine all adverse claims thereto subject to the existing Parcel One Excepted Interests, Parcel Two Excepted Interests, Parcel Three Excepted Interests, Parcel Four Excepted Interests, Parcel Five Excepted Interests and Parcel Six Excepted Interests;
(2) That no valid judgment has heretofore been entered as to the Property under the authority of the act entitled "[A]n act to provide for the establishment an quieting of title to real property in the case ofloss or destruction of public records" approved June 16, 1906, and as amended and most recently codified in CCP Section 751.02, et seq, that no valid judgment has been entered in any action authorized by Chapter 3.5 of Part 2, Title 10 of the CCP;
(3) That plaintiff has complied with the provisions of the Destroyed Land Records Act, CCP Section 751.02, et seq; and
(4) That the property descriptions attached as Exhibit A to the proposed Judgment are true and correct property descriptions of Parcels One through Six.

Counsel for plaintiff shall prepare the proposed Judgment and submit same to the Court for review and signature.
